text: It seems clear from the context that some words have dropped out. The Greek text has, ‘and come he might not, because of the snow. So he moved camp and went into the country of Galaad’. But the passage may originally have indicated what reasons Tryphon had for abandoning his campaign in Judaea.

‘After the tyrannous actions of Tryphon’; literally (in the Greek text), ‘because all the actions of Tryphon were seizures’. The meaning is perhaps that Simon now recognized all the actions of Tryphon as usurpations of power; it is evident from what follows that he was not sure of his position; had the Jews, by their support of a pretender, forfeited the privileges granted in 11.33-37?
Josephus is perhaps right in reading ‘Gazara’ (less than twenty miles from Jerusalem) instead of ‘Gaza’; cf. 14.7.
